chief_complaint: Two patients, ages 35 and 42, presented with sudden onset of weakness and speech difficulties.
history: Patient 1: A 35-year-old man with a 5-year history of hypereosinophilic syndrome (HES) presented with sudden onset of right-sided weakness and aphasia. Patient 2: A 42-year-old man with a 10-year history of HES presented with sudden onset of left-sided weakness and dysarthria. Both patients had a history of recurrent eosinophilic pneumonia and cardiac involvement.
exam: Vital signs: Patient 1 - BP 120/80, HR 80, RR 20; Patient 2 - BP 130/90, HR 90, RR 22. Physical examination: Patient 1 - Right-sided weakness, aphasia; Patient 2 - Left-sided weakness, dysarthria.
labs: ECG: Patient 1 - Normal; Patient 2 - Normal. Bloods: Patient 1 - Eosinophil count 25,000/mm^3; Patient 2 - Eosinophil count 30,000/mm^3. Imaging: Patient 1 - CT scan showing left middle cerebral artery territory infarct; Patient 2 - CT scan showing right middle cerebral artery territory infarct.
